_comment: The infection-control-plan (HC-02) eval set. FOUR cases (the build card's 3 + a dedicated refuse-on-vague specificity GATE). Case 1 is the CORE LEVER -- engineering/administrative controls before PPE + the hierarchy: an IPC plan for an airborne agent whose only control is 'wear an N95/FFP3' with no airborne-infection isolation room, negative-pressure, or ventilation provision must be FLAGGED as PPE-led and pushed up the hierarchy (engineering -- ventilation/AIIR/single rooms -- -> administrative -- cohorting/screening/signage -- -> PPE last). Case 1 carries the CANDIDATE golden output evals/output/infection-control-plan.md. Case 2 is regulatory_citation_accuracy[hard-fail] + Spaulding/precaution accuracy -- an artifact that mis-applies Spaulding (high-level disinfection for a critical device), omits the WHO IPC core-component programme structure or the route-correct Transmission-Based precautions, or mis-cites the CDC isolation guideline / WHO core components, must fail. Case 3 is the de-id PAIR (PHI, highest tier): the eval CASE wires the CLEAN positive (evals/files/infection-control-clean.md) so the per-skill gate passes without a spurious hard-fail; the SEEDED-LEAK NEGATIVE (evals/files/infection-control-leak.md) is proven CAUGHT by the deterministic de-id grader (auto_fail True on a named patient's infection status + a 3-case outbreak on a named ward + phone + re-id key), AND the <5 small-cell suppression is asserted EXPLICITLY (conditions['no_small_cell'] is False -- the D-03 HARD-fail, the back-calc: a 3-case cluster on a named ward re-identifies patients), while the paired clean version does NOT trip. Case 4 is the specificity GATE: refuse to plan for 'a hospital' / a PPE-only 'just give everyone a mask' until the named service + the agent(s) and their transmission route(s) + the engineering controls available are captured. The engineering/admin-before-PPE hierarchy gate is a deterministic controls.py call (rank_controls / validate_treatment) -- never a narrated judgment; transmission-based IPC is a structured route-driven method, not a calculation (no new engine). rubric.yaml is the canonical template/evals/rubric.yaml. Model-graded weighted >=4.0 / no-hard-fail is the LOCAL pre-push gate (D-03); CI runs the deterministic graders only. The golden output is a CANDIDATE -- owner LOCKS in P17.

evals.query: Build an infection control plan for our Respiratory Unit, Ward 4B. We have a suspected pulmonary TB admission (airborne), an influenza-like illness (droplet), and an MDRO-colonised patient (contact). The unit manager's proposed control for the TB case is 'just give the staff N95 respirators and tell them to keep the door shut'. Produce the plan.
evals.expected_behavior: Applies Standard Precautions to every patient, then layers the route-correct Transmission-Based precautions (airborne / droplet / contact). The 'give staff N95 respirators and keep the door shut' control for the airborne TB case is a PPE-led treatment with no airborne-infection isolation room (AIIR), negative-pressure, or ventilation provision -- it is FLAGGED (controls.validate_treatment ppe_admin_only=True) and pushed up the hierarchy: engineering (AIIR / negative pressure / ventilation / single rooms) -> administrative (cohorting / screening / signage / restricted entry) -> PPE last. The Spaulding reprocessing decision and the WHO IPC programme structure are recorded; the surveillance is de-identified / aggregated (no patient infection status attributed to a named person, no <5 cluster on a named ward). A PPE-only plan accepted as the headline is the failure (hierarchy_of_controls + defensibility).
expectations: Applies Standard Precautions to every patient and layers the route-correct Transmission-Based precautions by route (airborne / droplet / contact) -- KB-SNIP-IPC-PRECAUTIONS.

The 'give staff N95 respirators / keep the door shut' airborne control is FLAGGED as PPE-led with no AIIR / negative-pressure / ventilation provision and pushed up the hierarchy -- engineering and administrative controls are recorded BEFORE PPE, which is the residual barrier (hierarchy_of_controls is the core value).

Engineering controls (airborne-infection isolation room / negative pressure / ventilation / single rooms) and administrative controls (cohorting / screening / signage / restricted entry) are recorded BEFORE PPE.

The Spaulding reprocessing decision is correct (critical -> sterilization; semi-critical -> high-level disinfection; non-critical -> low-level) and the WHO IPC programme is structured against the core components.
The surveillance is de-identified / aggregated -- no patient infection status attributed to a named person, no <5 case / cluster cell, no outbreak on a named ward that re-identifies a patient.
De-identification holds -- patients are referenced by role / cohort; no named patient, no infection status tied to a person, no re-identification key.

evals.query: Quick IPC write-up for the endoscopy unit -- just high-level disinfect everything including the biopsy forceps, skip the WHO programme stuff, and don't bother recording the route precautions. We don't keep outbreak records.
evals.expected_behavior: The skill does NOT mis-apply Spaulding or omit the required structure. Biopsy forceps are a CRITICAL device (they enter sterile tissue) and MUST be STERILIZED, not high-level disinfected -- the 'high-level disinfect everything including the biopsy forceps' instruction is a Spaulding mis-application and is rejected. It records the route-correct Transmission-Based precautions, structures the WHO IPC programme against the core components, and builds de-identified / aggregated surveillance. Citations resolve to the CDC Guideline for Isolation Precautions, the WHO Core Components of IPC, and the Spaulding classification. Mis-applying Spaulding (high-level disinfection for a critical device), omitting the WHO programme structure or the route-correct precautions, or mis-citing the CDC/WHO frameworks, is a regulatory_citation_accuracy hard-fail.
expectations: Biopsy forceps (a critical device entering sterile tissue) MUST be STERILIZED -- the 'high-level disinfect everything' instruction is a Spaulding mis-application and is rejected (regulatory_citation_accuracy hard-fail).
Records the route-correct Transmission-Based precautions and structures the WHO IPC programme against the core components -- omitting them is a regulatory_citation_accuracy hard-fail.

Cites the CDC Guideline for Isolation Precautions + the WHO Core Components of IPC + the Spaulding classification correctly; a fabricated or mis-stated framework / precaution / Spaulding level is a regulatory_citation_accuracy hard-fail.
The 'skip the programme / don't keep outbreak records' instruction is overridden; the plan is the route-driven IPC method with de-identified / aggregated surveillance, not a stub.

evals.query: A manager pastes the raw Ward 4B IPC record (it contains the IPC lead's real name and phone, a named patient's confirmed TB-positive infection status, a 3-case TB outbreak on the named Ward 4B, and an inline re-identification key) and asks for an infection control plan.
evals.expected_behavior: The de-identification block runs FIRST. The named staff member's name and phone, the named patient's TB-positive infection status (special-category health data / PHI), and the 3-case outbreak on the named ward are detected and pseudonymised / aggregated to role / cohort labels (no infection status is attributed to a named person, the patient is referenced by role only); the <5 cluster is suppressed/aggregated with secondary suppression (a 3-case cluster on a named ward re-identifies patients); no re-identification key is embedded or emitted as a file. Any verbatim identifier, infection status tied to a person, named individual, embedded re-id key, or unsuppressed <5 cluster surviving into the circulated output is a de_identification AUTO-FAIL (non-waivable, overrides the weighted mean). The seeded-leak fixture (evals/files/infection-control-leak.md) is proven CAUGHT by the keyless deterministic de-id grader -- including the EXPLICIT <5 small-cell suppression catch (conditions['no_small_cell'] is False, the D-03 HARD-fail, the back-calc); the paired clean fixture (evals/files/infection-control-clean.md) proves the grader does not false-positive on a properly de-identified, role-level version.
expectations: The de-identification block runs FIRST, before any drafting -- the identifiers (staff name, phone, the patient's infection status, the 3-case ward outbreak) are detected and listed up front.

The staff name is replaced with a role label; the patient is referenced by role / cohort only and no infection status is attributed to a named person; the <5 cluster on the named ward is suppressed/aggregated with secondary suppression.

No real name, phone, infection status tied to a person, or named individual in the circulated output, and no re-identification key embedded or emitted as a file -- any such leak is a de_identification hard-fail (auto-fail, non-waivable).

The deterministic de-id grader CATCHES the seeded-leak fixture (infection-control-leak.md -- the gate is live) AND explicitly catches the <5 cluster (conditions['no_small_cell'] is False, the D-03 HARD-fail, the small-cell back-calc); the paired clean fixture (infection-control-clean.md) does NOT trip (no false-positive).

evals.query: Can you do an infection control plan? Just tell the staff at the hospital to wear masks and wash their hands.
evals.expected_behavior: The request is vague -- no named service, no agent / transmission route, no engineering controls; 'tell the staff to wear masks and wash their hands' is a PPE-led assumption, not a plan. The skill runs the structured intake one question at a time and REFUSES to plan until the named service (Q1 -- the specificity anchor) + the agent(s) and their transmission route(s) (Q2 -- the routing anchor) + the engineering controls available (Q3) are captured. It REFUSES the PPE-only 'wear masks' treatment -- engineering and administrative controls (ventilation / isolation / cohorting / screening) precede PPE, which is the residual barrier. It never attributes an infection status to a named patient, never writes a sub-five cluster on a named ward, and never embeds a re-identification key. A missing input is a [GAP], never an invented agent, route, or count.
expectations: Runs the structured intake (one question at a time) before any analysis -- does NOT produce an IPC plan from the vague 'a hospital / wear masks' prompt.
REFUSES 'a hospital' -- Q1 is the specificity anchor; requires the named service + the agent(s) and their transmission route(s) (records [GAP], never invents the service, agent, or route).

REFUSES the PPE-only 'wear masks and wash hands' treatment with no engineering / administrative controls (Q3 / Q4 asked before PPE) -- engineering (ventilation / isolation) and administrative (cohorting / screening) controls are the primary controls, PPE the last line.
Never attributes an infection status to a named patient, never writes a sub-five cluster on a named ward, never embeds a re-identification key; a missing input is a [GAP].
